_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A1/002 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 002 · My Weekend

npc.text: Hi, Anna. How was your weekend?

routes.meaning: Anna says her weekend was great and she went to the zoo with her family.
examples: It was great! I went to the zoo with my family.
It was great! I went to the zoo.
I went to the zoo with my family.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “It was great! I went to the zoo with my family.”

routes.meaning: Anna says she went to the zoo.
examples: It was good. I went to the zoo.
I went to the zoo with my mom.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “It was great! I went to the zoo with my family.”

partial.message: Tell Tom about your weekend: “It was great! I went to the zoo with my family.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked about your weekend. Say “It was great! I went to the zoo with my family.”

npc.text: Wow! What animals did you see?

routes.meaning: Anna saw lions and monkeys, and the monkeys were funny.
examples: I saw lions and monkeys. The monkeys were funny.
I saw monkeys. They were funny.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I saw lions and monkeys. The monkeys were funny.”

routes.meaning: Anna names an animal she saw.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I saw lions and monkeys. The monkeys were funny.”

partial.message: Name the animals: “I saw lions and monkeys. The monkeys were funny.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked what you saw. Say “I saw lions and monkeys. The monkeys were funny.”

routes.meaning: Anna saw elephants and they were very big.
examples: Yes, I did. They were very big.
Yes, I did. They were big.
Yes! The elephants were very big.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes, I did. They were very big.”

examples: No, I did not see elephants.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes, I did. They were very big.”

partial.message: Did you see elephants? Say “Yes, I did. They were very big.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked about elephants. Try “Yes, I did. They were very big.”

npc.text: That's okay! Elephants are very big. Maybe next time.

routes.meaning: Anna agrees to see them next time.

off_topic.message: Maybe next time. Say “Yes, next time!”

npc.text: I stayed home because it was rainy.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what Tom did at home.
examples: What did you do at home?
What did you do at home then?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What did you do at home?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks why Tom stayed home.
examples: Why did you not go out?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What did you do at home?”

partial.message: Ask Tom: “What did you do at home?”

off_topic.message: Tom stayed home. Ask “What did you do at home?”

npc.text: Because it rained all day! So I stayed inside.

routes.meaning: Anna asks what Tom did at home.
examples: What did you do at home?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “What did you do at home?”

partial.message: Ask Tom: “What did you do at home?”

off_topic.message: It rained all day. Ask “What did you do at home?”

npc.text: I read books and played games.

routes.meaning: Anna says that sounds fun too.
Anna asks what games Tom played.

partial.message: Respond to Tom: “That sounds fun too!”

off_topic.message: Tom read and played games. Say “That sounds fun too!”

npc.text: I played a car game. It was so much fun!

routes.meaning: Anna says that sounds fun too.

off_topic.message: Tom's game was fun. Say “That sounds fun too!”

npc.text: Thanks, Anna! Let's go to the zoo together next time.
